3.narrative: From a starting price of 132.3484, the asset experienced a significant decline to an end price of 84.3718, resulting in a negative return of -36.2502%. The period was marked by elevated volatility, with an annualized volatility of 0.624476 and a maximum drawdown reaching -47.0175%, indicating substantial price fluctuations and risk exposure. The largest down day occurred on 2026-02-05, with a sharp drop of -15.0407% closing at 78.1874, accompanied by high trading volume of 10,561,367,080 shares; this day coincided with reports of tech layoffs and AI revenue concerns impacting market sentiment [3]. Conversely, the largest up day was on 2026-02-06, with an 11.8655% gain closing at 87.4648 and an exceptionally high volume of 11,684,451,362 shares, coinciding with multiple news items including a monthly business update and announcements related to staking rewards on the Solana ETF [1][2]. Another notable up day on 2026-02-25 saw an 11.2342% increase closing at 87.9156 with volume of 5,782,225,888 shares, occurring alongside legal developments such as the expansion of a lawsuit involving Solana executives [14][15]. Average daily volume increased by 4.5004% to over 4.3 billion shares, reflecting heightened trading activity during this period. Overall, the price action and volume spikes appear linked to a mix of regulatory, legal, and operational news events, though the precise impact of each driver on price movements remains subject to interpretation.
tldr: Highest volume spike was on 2026-02-06 with volume 11,668,451,362.
3.conclusion: Over this period, the asset ended lower (-36.25%) with peak drawdown of -47.02%, indicating meaningful price swings during the window.
